title: Duchesnea chrysantha
intro: Duchesnea chrysantha, commonly known as the yellow-petaled cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the rose family, native to Europe and Asia. The health benefits of Duchesnea chrysantha include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including urinary tract infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Duchesnea chrysantha is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.

This family comprises over 2,000 species of plants, including roses, apricots, and pears. Duchesnea chrysantha is a perennial plant that produces bright yellow flowers and is native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It is a member of the subfamily Rosoideae, which also includes the genus Fragaria, commonly known as strawberries.
